Zhavoronkov anticipates that within the next two to three years, pharmacological solutions will be developed to help individuals stay younger and healthier longer.

Manchester City will not allow Valencia to withdraw from their £24 million deal to sign striker Alvaro Negredo, despite reports suggesting Valencia might want to do so to fund a potential move for Radamel Falcao. Negredo's reluctance to celebrate a recent goal has fueled speculation about his future, with Valencia facing financial difficulties that have led them to consider offloading Negredo to afford Falcao's transfer. Scouts from Manchester City were also present at FC Porto's surprising Champions League victory over Bayern Munich, primarily interested in Porto's left-back Alex Sandro.

Former tennis player Greg Rusedski supports Aljaz Bedene’s bid to represent Great Britain in the Davis Cup, despite an International Tennis Federation (ITF) ruling that blocks Bedene due to his past representation of Slovenia. Bedene, currently ranked 99th in the world and holding a British passport since last month, argues his case on the basis that his citizenship was pending when the ITF changed its rules. Rusedski, who previously switched nationalities himself, believes Bedene's inclusion could positively impact British tennis by motivating other players to improve their rankings.

Milinda Gunasekera, a 32-year-old Perth resident, pleaded guilty to groping a female passenger on a Qantas flight after consuming a bottle of vodka at Auckland Airport. The incident occurred while Gunasekera was en route from Chile to Perth via Auckland, and he reportedly cannot recall the event due to extreme intoxication. Since the incident, Gunasekera has acknowledged his alcoholism, undergone a rehabilitation program, and remained sober, showing remorse for his actions.

Chelsea's defender Wallace, on loan at Vitesse Arnhem, was released without charges after being questioned by the police under suspicion of a sexual offense. As a result, Vitesse announced that Wallace would not play in their upcoming match against Excelsior and would receive a maximum fine. The Dutch club stated they have no further details to provide about the incident, which remains under investigation by the police, though it is suspected to be related to a sexual offense.

Manchester United's striker Robin van Persie, currently sidelined with an ankle injury, is drawing interest from Italian clubs Juventus and Inter Milan due to his potential as a short-term quality option. With only one year left on his contract and a decline in performance despite scoring 10 goals this season, Van Persie may become a less central figure at United, who are considering strengthening their attack by signing two new forwards this summer. Other clubs interested in the Dutch forward include Real Madrid, Paris Saint-Germain, and Barcelona, while United are also exploring the possibility of signing PSG's Edinson Cavani.

Lidia Quilligana, a nanny in Danbury, Connecticut, was arrested after a hidden camera captured footage of her allegedly abusing a 3-year-old girl in her care, including choking, beating, and burning the child's limbs on a hot stove. The parents had installed the camera due to previous suspicions of mistreatment. Despite her defense attorney claiming the charges are false and presenting testimonials from other families vouching for her trustworthiness, Quilligana faces serious charges of first-degree assault of a minor and criminal mischief, with bail set at $1 million.

FLIR Systems Inc., based in Oregon, has agreed to pay $9.5 million to settle bribery charges involving lavish gifts and trips provided to Saudi government officials, as alleged by the Securities and Exchange Commission. The company admitted that its inadequate financial controls allowed two Dubai-based employees to give luxury watches and arrange international travel for officials, resulting in over $7 million in sales influenced by these acts of corruption. As part of the settlement, FLIR must now report to the SEC on its compliance efforts under the Foreign Corrupt Practices Act for two years.

Carlos Tevez, the Juventus striker, has denied claims that he plans to rejoin his boyhood club Boca Juniors this summer, stating that he has yet to finalize any decisions regarding his future. Despite celebrating a recent goal with a gesture reminiscent of one he used while playing for Boca Juniors, Tevez insists that such celebrations were part of a promise to supporters and unrelated to any potential move. He emphasizes his focus on performing well for Juventus, currently experiencing an "extraordinary" period, and declines to comment further on any potential departures.

Matthew Kenney, a 34-year-old from Florida, was arrested after running naked through traffic in Fort Lauderdale while under the influence of Flakka, a potent designer drug. Kenney believed imaginary killers had stolen his clothes and were pursuing him, leading him to risk running through traffic to escape. He was hospitalized for a psychiatric evaluation after telling police he preferred death over capture by the perceived assailants. This incident highlights the dangerous effects of Flakka, a synthetic cathinone known for causing hallucinations and psychotic behavior, whose use in Florida has been increasing significantly over recent years.

Roxy Walsh, a Queensland resident, found a gold ring while snorkeling at Finns Beach Club in Bali. The ring, which features a possible family crest and an engraving reading "Darling Joe, Happy 70th Birthday 2009, Love Jenny," was posted on Facebook by Walsh in an attempt to locate its rightful owner. Her post, widely shared on social media including by the travel blog The Bali Bible, has garnered over 23,000 shares, expanding the search for Joe and Jenny globally.

Tottenham Hotspur's goalkeeper Hugo Lloris will miss the upcoming Premier League match against Burnley due to a knee injury sustained during a game against Leicester City. Manager Mauricio Pochettino stated that Lloris remains in recovery, with Michel Vorm set to take his place. Additionally, there is uncertainty surrounding striker Roberto Soldado's participation, though Pochettino expressed optimism that the England players who played in the recent international matches would return with boosted confidence.

Daniel Steele, a 25-year-old man from North Carolina, has been arrested and charged with the murder of his pregnant girlfriend, Kimberly Dianne Richardson, who was six months pregnant. The fatal shooting took place in Raleigh around 9:40 pm on a Saturday, leading to Richardson's death early Sunday morning, while their unborn daughter survived and is under care at WakeMed hospital. The incident occurred in the 3600 block of Sumner Boulevard, near Triangle Town Center.

Liam Stewart, son of rock star Rod Stewart and model Rachel Hunter, was expected to debut for Great Britain's senior ice hockey team at the World Championships in Holland but his hopes were dashed due to a shoulder injury sustained during a playoff series. He was replaced in the squad by Craig Peacock of the Belfast Giants by head coach Pete Russell. Coach Russell expressed disappointment over Stewart’s absence due to the injury and highlighted Peacock's talent, emphasizing that the replacement was a result of a difficult initial selection decision.

A healthcare worker who recently returned to Australia from Liberia is under isolation at Canberra Hospital due to symptoms suggestive of Ebola, including fever and stomach upset. Although the woman did not treat Ebola patients during her work at a clinic in Liberia, she has been placed in an isolation ward as a precautionary measure, with medical staff wearing full protective gear. Health officials emphasize that the risk of Ebola is extremely low and reassure the public that there is no significant risk to the community, given the nature of Ebola transmission and the hospital's stringent protocols.

Celtic FC has reached out to the Scottish Football Association to clarify the refereeing decisions, particularly regarding a missed penalty and red card opportunity involving a clear handball by Josh Meekings during their Scottish Cup semi-final loss to Inverness Caledonian Thistle. Despite expressing frustration over the officiating, Celtic acknowledged Inverness's historic achievement by reaching their first-ever Scottish Cup final. The match saw Celtic take an early lead but eventually lose 3-2 after extra time, ending their chances of securing a domestic treble this season.

During a brunch at Bocca East in New York, Nicholas Dematteis, 39, became enraged after waiting over an hour for his meal and demanded a free meal due to slow service. He then assaulted the restaurant manager, allegedly grabbing him by the neck and slamming him against a bar and into an elderly woman outside the restaurant. Dematteis was arrested and charged with assault; this incident follows a previous arrest in January for breaking into his girlfriend's apartment.

Gitte Denteneer, a mother dining at the Rubens restaurant in Leuven, Belgium, was shocked to find a 50 cent charge on her bill for warming her baby's bottle. The incident sparked outrage on social media after Denteneer shared a picture of the bill, highlighting what many consider an unusually stingy practice. The restaurant chain, Horeca Vlaanderen, later clarified that such charges are typically rare and only apply if customers are informed beforehand.

Manchester City and Chelsea are competing to sign West Ham's impressive left-back, Aaron Cresswell, who has performed well in the Premier League this season. Both clubs aim to bolster their English player quotas; City due to the potential departure of James Milner, while Chelsea looks at Cresswell as an alternative to Filipe Luis, considering their own English quota requirements. However, West Ham may resist selling Cresswell, given his solid performance and long-term contract.

Manchester United has presented David de Gea with an offer worth £200,000 per week to prevent him from leaving the club, making it the highest salary for a goalkeeper globally. Manager Louis Van Gaal acknowledged the generous offer but expressed uncertainty about De Gea's decision, with fears that the Spanish player might choose to return to Real Madrid as a free agent next summer. Despite the club's efforts to retain him, there is a belief within the team that De Gea could depart to replace Iker Casillas at Real Madrid.

Ian Bell, a key player for England and Warwickshire, has extended his contract with the club until the end of the 2017 season. Having started with Warwickshire's youth teams in 1999, Bell, now 32, has played significant roles in English cricket, participating in 105 Test matches and 161 one-day internationals. Over his career, he has contributed to numerous successes at Warwickshire, winning titles such as the County Championship in 2004 and 2012, and expressing his joy at continuing his service to the club.

Tyler MacNiven, winner of The Amazing Race's ninth season, has created an elaborate "save the date" video with his fiancée, Kelly Hennigan, using part of his $1 million prize. The three-minute film, reminiscent of an action movie, features the couple battling disguised friends as villains before escaping a massive explosion, all to announce their wedding on September 26, 2015. The video culminates with MacNiven and Hennigan, dressed in formal attire, enjoying a scenic boat ride under San Francisco’s Golden Gate Bridge.

AC/DC drummer Phil Rudd, aged 60, has pleaded guilty in a New Zealand court to charges of threatening to kill his personal assistant and possessing drugs. Arrested in November last year, Rudd initially denied the allegations but changed his plea right before the trial began, admitting to threatening to kill his assistant over phone calls due to the poor performance of his solo album. With potential jail time of up to seven years, Rudd remains on bail until his sentencing hearing in June, with his lawyer aiming to avoid a conviction despite the guilty plea.

Rebecca Grant, a 40-year-old woman from Biddeford, Maine, was arrested for alleged drunken behavior and subsequently faced additional charges after violently resisting arrest. Grant allegedly head-butted a deputy, bit the police cruiser's upholstery, and left bite marks on the vehicle's paint during her transport to jail, causing $500 in damages. She also verbally threatened to kill deputies and their families, leading to charges including violating bail conditions and criminal mischief.

A newborn baby girl, nearly full-term, was found dead in a garbage can outside a sorority house at Muskingum University in Ohio. According to the coroner, the infant was born alive before being suffocated; her unnamed mother, a student at the university, has not been charged. This incident is the second such tragedy at the school, where another abandoned baby died in 2002. Ohio's Safe Haven Law allows mothers to anonymously surrender newborns at hospitals or fire stations without facing legal consequences, provided there is no sign of abuse or neglect.

Brazilian football legend Ronaldo expressed interest in playing alongside Cristiano Ronaldo if he were to resume his career with the North American League side Fort Lauderdale Strikers, which he co-owns. Although acknowledging that his playing days are over, Ronaldo praised the current Real Madrid star and also commended Lionel Messi, highlighting their dominance and goal-scoring prowess in world football.

Dan Price, CEO of Seattle-based Gravity Payments, has implemented a $70,000 minimum wage across his credit card processing firm, significantly reducing his own salary from $1 million to align with that of his lowest-paid workers. This decision, made after studying the impact of pay on employee happiness and witnessing financial struggles among his peers, will affect over half of his 120 employees. Price plans to cover the increased payroll costs by reinvesting up to 80% of the company’s projected $2.2 million annual profit, a move highlighting the stark contrast between executive pay and employee compensation in today's corporate environment.

Dikembe Mutombo, an eight-time NBA All-Star renowned for his shot-blocking skills and iconic finger-wagging gesture, has been inducted into the Naismith Memorial Basketball Hall of Fame. Known for his defensive prowess, Mutombo played in the NBA from 1991 to 2009, earning four NBA Defensive Player of the Year awards and leading the league in blocked shots for five consecutive seasons. Alongside Mutombo, other notable figures like coach John Calipari and players Spencer Haywood, JoJo White, and Lisa Leslie were also elected to the Class of 2015, which will be enshrined on September 11 in Springfield, Massachusetts.

Parents Rachel Jaajaa, 22, and Aaron Tate, 29, face charges of child endangering after they could not agree on who should care for their three-month-old daughter over the weekend. Tate allegedly left the baby alone at a McDonald's in Elyria, Ohio, while Jaajaa refused to take her because it was Tate's 'turn.' The baby was discovered by a patron who called 911; the parents were subsequently arrested and their infant is now being cared for by Jaajaa's sister.

Derby County is considering Real Madrid coach Paul Clement as a replacement for their current manager, Steve McClaren, who may soon leave for Newcastle United. With the prospect of McClaren's departure, Derby has initiated the search for a new manager and recognizes Clement as a strong candidate, given his impressive coaching credentials and experience under Carlo Ancelotti. Should McClaren lead Derby to the Premier League, he might choose to stay, but the club is already preparing for his possible exit by exploring options like Clement, who would need to consider leaving his position at Real Madrid.

Didier Drogba announced he plans to continue his football career beyond this season, contrary to speculation about retirement, stating, "It's not my last season." Despite a reduced playing time due to competition from new signings Diego Costa and Loic Remy, Drogba remains committed to staying fit and ready for any opportunity. The 37-year-old veteran emphasized the importance of maintaining focus as Chelsea approaches the end of their Premier League title race, noting the potential volatility of the competition despite their current lead.

Seven-year-old India Mayhew died after suffering severe head injuries when the horse she was riding bolted during a holiday in Matheran, India. The incident occurred on the second day of their trip, with India riding only a few meters ahead of her father and a staff member, who managed to rush her to a nearby health center but could not save her. The family, including India's mother and siblings, were staying at a five-star hotel in Mumbai before visiting Matheran, and police registered the case as an accidental death based on the family's statement.

Liverpool Mayor Joe Anderson, aged 56, received £4,500 annually from Chesterfield High School despite no longer performing any duties there, justifying the payments by arguing they provided the school with 'kudos.' After the school ceased these payments in 2011 due to his elevated position as Mayor, Anderson attempted to sue for discrimination, citing his belief in public service. However, a judge ruled that continuing the payments constituted a misallocation of public funds and that the school was justified in ending them, noting that Anderson failed to demonstrate any specific benefits the school gained from the payments.

Jermain Defoe, despite Sunderland's ongoing struggle for Premier League survival, was seen enjoying a game of basketball, posting a photo on Instagram. Sunderland is battling to avoid relegation, having won only once in their last nine matches and recently suffering a 4-1 defeat to Crystal Palace. The team's challenging schedule ahead, coupled with their current precarious position, makes every match crucial for their survival, especially given their next game against Everton could determine whether they remain three points above the drop zone.

Sebastien Pocognoli expressed confusion and frustration over losing his playing time at West Bromwich Albion under new manager Tony Pulis, despite having performed well under his previous manager, Alan Irvine. In an interview with La Meuse, Pocognoli stated that he is unsure of the reasons behind his reduced game time and emphasized his desire to remain in the Premier League, while keeping his options open for a potential move to a different league if necessary. The situation highlights the challenges faced by players adjusting to new managers and their strategies.

Everton manager Roberto Martinez defended Ross Barkley after the midfielder missed a crucial penalty in their 1-0 win against Burnley. Despite Barkley's lower success rate compared to veteran Leighton Baines, who was present and has a high conversion rate, Martinez supported Barkley's decision to take the penalty. The miss was reminiscent of a previous incident where another player, Kevin Mirallas, also missed a penalty he had taken from Baines, illustrating the club's recent struggles with penalty-taking consistency.

Phil Haste, a 60-year-old businessman from Torquay, received a £50 fine for not displaying a tax disc, a practice made illegal six months prior to the fine. He also faced accusations of not showing a pay and display ticket, despite having a valid parking permit visible. Haste has refused to pay the fine, calling it a "diabolical" decision, and plans to appeal or go to court, claiming the council's enforcement officers lack proper knowledge and may be issuing fines indiscriminately to increase revenue.

Iker Casillas, Real Madrid's goalkeeper, has commented that the persistent scrutiny over his position as the team's first-choice keeper is "morbid," following a clean sheet against Rayo Vallecano. Despite criticism from fans and press this season, Casillas stated that he is content with his performance and emphasized that the team's focus should remain on winning games rather than dealing with external pressures. He also clarified that any decision to bench him would be aimed at winning, not just protecting him from fan disapproval.

Moeen Ali, a prominent English cricketer and lifelong Liverpool fan, has been appointed as an honorary ambassador for Liverpool's fan club in Pakistan, known as Pak Reds. Ali, who has Pakistani heritage and will play in England's upcoming Test matches against the West Indies, expressed his excitement about joining the association. Alongside Ali, other notable ambassadors for the Pak Reds include former Liverpool doctor Dr. Zaf Iqbal, tennis player Aisam-Ul-Haq, and BBC presenter Noreen Khan.

In Alderholt, Dorset, an anonymous individual or group delivered chocolate eggs to every household in the village of approximately 3,000 people over Easter. The surprise delivery left residents delighted, with many taking to social media to express their joy at this unprecedented act of kindness. Village residents praised the gesture as a "wonderful act of random kindness," marveling at the scale of the operation required to deliver eggs to each home.

The BBC is set to air a unique, two-hour documentary that captures a canal boat journey along the Kennet and Avon Canal at a leisurely pace, with no narration or additional sound effects, aiming to offer viewers a tranquil visual and auditory experience. Shot continuously from a high-definition camera on the boat's front, the film includes minimal excitement such as a swan overtaking the narrowboat or avoiding passing vessels, emphasizing the serene beauty of the waterway. Producer Luke Korzun Martin describes the goal as creating a mesmerizing, slow-moving "moving painting" as part of the BBC Four's "Goes Slow" series.

Gareth Bale, despite being sidelined due to a calf injury sustained in Real Madrid's match against Malaga, showed support for his team by posting a motivational message on Facebook ahead of their crucial Champions League quarter-final against Atletico Madrid. The Welsh forward expressed his desire to return to full fitness quickly while wishing his teammates good luck. Manager Carlo Ancelotti remains confident about fielding a strong lineup, despite missing key players including Bale, Karim Benzema, and Luka Modric, stating that the team has enough options to maintain their competitive edge.

A rapidly spreading wildfire in Southern California, ignited by a cooking stove, led to the evacuation of approximately 300 homes on Saturday evening. By Sunday, firefighters had managed to contain 35% of the fire, which had grown to cover 1.6 square miles, thanks to cooperative weather conditions and the efforts of around 500 firefighters. Despite the severity of the fire, which burned through thick vegetation in the Prado Dam Flood Control Basin, no injuries or property damages were reported.

Charlene Mess, 48, was arrested for the alleged murder of her husband, Douglas Mess, 52, at their New York farm after he was reported missing and his body was later discovered buried under a pile of manure. The search, which involved more than 25 state troopers and local volunteers, lasted seven hours before locating the body, during which time Mess remained on the property. Charged with second-degree murder, she is being held without bail as authorities continue to investigate the motive behind the killing.

During the Chinese Grand Prix, Jenson Button was penalized with a five-second time addition and received two Super Licence penalty points for crashing into Pastor Maldonado’s Lotus car while battling for 13th place. This collision caused Maldonado to spin out and retire from the race, while Button managed to finish 13th but was later demoted to 14th due to the penalty. The incident highlighted another challenging weekend for McLaren, as both Button and teammate Fernando Alonso struggled towards the lower ranks of the race.

The parents of Michael Brown, an 18-year-old black teenager fatally shot by white police officer Darren Wilson in Ferguson, Missouri, in 2014, announced plans to file a wrongful death civil lawsuit against the city of Ferguson on the following day. The lawsuit, which was previously hinted at by the family's attorneys in March, is aimed at addressing the wrongful death of Michael Brown and follows extensive protests and the rise of the 'Black Lives Matter' movement. Despite a grand jury and the U.S. Justice Department declining to prosecute Officer Wilson, the latter's actions have been heavily criticized, leading to a Justice Department report highlighting racial bias within the Ferguson Police Department and a problematic court system targeting black residents.

Chris Smalling, a key defender for Manchester United, recently joined Twitter and posted his first photo of a barbecue with his girlfriend. United are set to face Manchester City at Old Trafford, with a victory potentially extending their lead in the race for a top-four finish. Additionally, teammate Adnan Januzaj also joined Twitter, although he faces limited playing time under Louis van Gaal and may seek more opportunities elsewhere.

Sergio Barrientos-Hinojosa, an intoxicated Albuquerque man, used his 13-year-old son as a getaway driver after firing shots at a customer during an argument at a local gas station. Police apprehended Barrientos-Hinojosa shortly after the incident, during which he allegedly yelled racial slurs at officers and attempted to evade arrest by throwing his firearm into a nearby residential yard. The young boy was reportedly "shell-shocked" by the experience, and his father now faces charges including child abuse.

In response to a statewide ban on beef sales and consumption, police in Malegaon, Maharashtra, India, are demanding that cattle owners provide identity cards for their animals, including photographs and detailed physical descriptions. This initiative aims to help enforce the ban and address recent incidents of cow slaughter. Over 100 owners have already complied with the requirement, which comes alongside stricter penalties for cow slaughter and beef-related offenses, though buffalo slaughter remains permissible. Beef traders protested against the new regulations through a month-long strike, while red meat consumers argue that the government is overstepping its bounds by interfering with personal dietary choices.

In a match at Wheatsheaf Park, Chelsea and Tottenham Hotspur failed to bridge the gap to Manchester United, who lead the Under 21 league, as they drew 0-0. Despite both teams creating numerous chances, particularly Tottenham in the second half, neither managed to score. This outcome allowed Manchester United to maintain their lead, with Tottenham moving up to third place and Chelsea remaining in eighth. Notably, Tottenham's DeAndre Yedlin performed impressively at right-back, contributing significantly to the team's strong second-half performance.

Over 1,700 Chinese students from Yichuan Senior High School in Shaanxi province took an exam outdoors due to insufficient indoor space, with the initiative coming from the students themselves. To ensure comfort and prevent dazzling from sunlight, regular announcements instructed students to periodically rotate their desks and chairs. This outdoor exam was seen as a unique experience that helped expand the students' horizons and reduce stress, while also testing their organizational skills.

The Coast Guard Cutter Boutwell arrived in San Diego carrying over 14 tons of cocaine, worth approximately $424 million, which was seized during 19 operations in the eastern Pacific Ocean near Central and South America. This seizure is part of an ongoing increase in drug interdictions involving U.S. and Canadian forces, who have confiscated more than 28 tons of cocaine valued at $848 million within the last six months, leading to 101 arrests and marking the highest success rate since 2009. The Boutwell’s previous significant seizure, in October, involved over 14 tons of cocaine.

Jose Mourinho has praised Eden Hazard, stating that the player is one of the three best in world football alongside Lionel Messi and Cristiano Ronaldo. Hazard, who scored the winning goal against Manchester United that extended Chelsea's lead in the Premier League, has shown both exceptional talent and humility according to his manager. Mourinho emphasizes Hazard's growing physical and mental strength, as well as his crucial role in helping Chelsea's team performance, especially during challenging matches.

Franz Beckenbauer, a legendary figure at Bayern Munich, suggests that Jurgen Klopp might be an ideal candidate to succeed Pep Guardiola at the club. Klopp has expressed his intention to leave Borussia Dortmund this summer after achieving two Bundesliga titles in his tenure. While acknowledging Guardiola’s current contract extension until 2016, Beckenbauer emphasizes that Klopp's managerial skills align well with Bayern's ethos. Additionally, Beckenbauer highlights the importance of a fully fit Bayern squad to challenge Barcelona in the Champions League, particularly stressing the need to neutralize Lionel Messi's influence.

Esteban Cambiasso, a former Argentina international and Leicester City midfielder, believes that helping Leicester avoid relegation from the Premier League would be a significant achievement in his career, comparing it to winning a trophy. Joining Leicester on a free transfer last year, Cambiasso acknowledges the challenge of staying in the Premier League, given Leicester's current position at the bottom of the table, seven points behind the safety zone. However, he remains optimistic about Leicester's chances, citing the remaining fixtures as opportunities to improve their standing.

Jonathan Trott's long-awaited return to Test cricket ended prematurely as he was dismissed for a duck after just three balls, caught at first slip by Darren Bravo during his opening partnership with Alastair Cook. This brief innings came after Trott's absence of 17 months due to a stress-related illness, which had prompted his withdrawal from the 2013 Ashes series. Despite being moved up to open alongside Cook as a strategic choice, Trott's dismissal left England struggling at 1-1 in their first innings against the West Indies in Antigua.

Shannon Hayes, a 12-year-old schoolgirl from Capel Iwan, Carmarthenshire, believes her hen has laid what could be the world's smallest chicken egg, measuring just 1.8 cm in length. This measurement is smaller than the previously reported smallest egg, which was 2.1 cm long. Shannon plans to keep the egg safely stored in her fridge while awaiting official verification for the Guinness World Records.

On Friday, six Leeds United players, including key members of the squad, claimed injuries and withdrew from the upcoming game against Charlton Athletic. This incident occurred amidst ongoing turmoil at the club, with the players' claims coming as a surprise to both head coach Neil Redfearn and the club's medical staff. The situation further complicates Redfearn's tenure, given that five of these players were signed by Massimo Cellino, the club's banned president, adding layers of uncertainty to the future leadership at Leeds.

Nicola Sturgeon, leader of the SNP, has undergone a significant style transformation, moving away from her previous boxy jackets and severe suits. Recently seen on her way to the Andrew Marr Show, she wore a flattering fuchsia column dress by Edinburgh label Totty Rocks, suggesting she has hired a stylist and possibly a personal shopper to enhance her image. This change marks a stark contrast to her earlier appearances, including a more casual look at a 2010 charity event and a dated hairstyle from the late 1990s.

John R. Lind, a 34-year-old hardware store employee in New Brighton, Minnesota, admitted to ejaculating in his female colleague Pat Maahs' coffee as a bizarre attempt to draw attention. Maahs initially suspected spoiled milk before learning the truth. In response to this incident, Maahs proposed new legislation to criminalize similar acts as felonies, feeling that current laws were insufficient. Lind was convicted of indecent exposure and will face sentencing on May 22.

Jamie Carragher has declared Chelsea captain John Terry as the best defender in Premier League history, praising his influence on the pitch despite playing alongside stars like Eden Hazard. Terry's standout performance in the recent 0-0 draw against Arsenal further cemented his reputation, with Carragher noting his brilliance even as Chelsea is often viewed as a defensively strong team. Additionally, Chelsea manager Jose Mourinho complimented Terry post-match, deeming it the best performance by the player in his entire career.

Detectives investigating the suspected murder of Deborah Steel, who ran the Royal Standard pub in Ely, Cambridgeshire, and disappeared in 1997, recently conducted a search by digging up the patio of the pub. Though no new evidence was found, the police reclassified the disappearance as a murder inquiry due to strong suspicions that she met an untimely death. The ongoing investigation involves several individuals on bail, with searches also previously conducted at a house where Ms. Steel once lived, reflecting the enduring mystery surrounding her case.

Manny Pacquiao, set to face undefeated boxer Floyd Mayweather in a $300 million bout on May 2 in Las Vegas, vows to bring an all-out "warrior" mentality into the ring, promising to fight as if it's his last match. Pacquiao, considered the underdog, asserts that his determination to win each round decisively will ensure there's no doubt about his victory on May 2. He confidently declares it's time for Mayweather to finally taste defeat.

The LAPD is investigating the vandalism of a police horse named Charly in Venice Beach, where an unidentified individual sprayed graffiti reading "RBS" on the horse during patrol. While the paint was easily removed, locals expressed concern over the cruelty and disrespect shown towards animals, and authorities hope to identify the culprit through the unique graffiti signature. The public is encouraged to assist in providing any information that could lead to the identification of the perpetrator, with contacts available through the LAPD or Crime Stoppers.

A 13-year-old boy from Medina, Ohio, allegedly stole $25,000 from his 83-year-old grandfather and distributed hundreds of dollars to his classmates at Claggett Middle School. After school, he continued handing out money at a friend's house, leading to a shopping spree among those who received the cash. Authorities have managed to recover about $7,000 so far and are investigating to determine who else was involved, with plans to charge the individuals responsible.

Bastia's president, Pierre-Marie Geronimi, has demanded the resignation of French League president Frederic Thiriez following an incident where Thiriez did not greet the players before Saturday's League Cup final. This demand comes after Thiriez's perceived slight towards Bastia, including not presenting them with their Ligue 2 champions' trophy in 2012. Bastia ultimately lost the match to Paris Saint-Germain with a score of 4-0, with goals from Zlatan Ibrahimovic and Edinson Cavani contributing to the decisive victory.

Hayleigh Mcbay, a 17-year-old Scottish student, attempted to play an April Fools' Day prank on her boyfriend David Clarke by pretending to break up with him via WhatsApp. However, David, catching onto the joke, responded by feigning his own desire to end their relationship. After realizing the joke was on her, Hayleigh shared the exchange on Twitter, where the tweet gained widespread attention, accumulating over 12,000 retweets and 11,000 likes, though it also drew some negative and sarcastic comments.

David Wihby, 61, who served as the state director for New Hampshire Senator Kelly Ayotte and was her longtime friend and second-in-command, resigned after being arrested for soliciting a prostitute. The arrest, which occurred on Friday in Nashua, led to Wihby stepping down on Saturday due to the nature of the misdemeanor charge and its implications for his position. Senator Ayotte expressed shock and sadness over the incident, acknowledging that Wihby, known for his extensive political career including roles as a Manchester alderman and Labor commissioner, had to resign "under these circumstances."

Arsenal manager Arsene Wenger disclosed in an interview with the official Arsenal Magazine that he doesn't finalize his starting lineup until the morning of matchdays, despite considering potential selections throughout the week. He weighs various factors, including player form and team balance, alongside medical assessments, to make his decision. This detailed process, combined with his intuitive judgment, has contributed to Wenger's success, yielding three Premier League titles and five FA Cups during his long tenure at Arsenal.

During a recent episode of Jeopardy, contestant Tom, a Freemason, gave an inappropriate response to a question about puberty, mistakenly answering "What is the age of consent?" This blunder caused significant discomfort among both the studio audience and viewers, who took to social media to express their shock and disapproval. The correct response should have been "What is puberty?", as the question referred to the legal age of adulthood under common law, not sexual maturity or consent.

Rafael Nadal kicked off his clay court season with a dominant 6-2, 6-1 victory over Lucas Pouille at the Monte Carlo Masters, showcasing a controlled performance despite not unleashing his full potential. Despite Pouille's occasional displays of skill, Nadal maintained dominance, breaking his opponent's serve multiple times and advancing confidently to the third round. This win marks an initial step towards rebuilding Nadal's confidence following recent defeats at the Indian Wells Masters and Miami Open.

Auckland coastguards responded to an unusual call about homemade boats participating in an annual fishing event, featuring vessels like a couch strapped to tires and a floating Subaru car. One of these makeshift boats capsized, prompting assistance from volunteer rescue units; fortunately, no injuries occurred. Despite the unconventional nature of the vessels, organizers emphasized safety, noting that participants wore life jackets and monitored weather conditions, while police issued warnings but did not press charges.

Navinder Sarao, a 36-year-old British trader, is accused of causing a $500 billion (approximately £500 billion) stock market crash on Wall Street in 2010 from his home in London. Prosecutors claim he earned £27 million from the 'Flash Crash' and boasted in an email that he could make £1 million in a week during volatile markets. Sarao was arraigned at Westminster Magistrates' Court but remains in custody due to bail conditions not being met, despite being granted bail with a surety of £5 million.

The official 30-second promotional video for the highly anticipated boxing match between Floyd Mayweather and Manny Pacquiao was released recently, showcasing both boxers walking towards each other in a desert setting near Las Vegas. The clip, which includes the MGM Grand and Las Vegas welcome sign in the background, features a commentary highlighting the historic nature of the event. The fight, scheduled for May 2nd, is projected to set new records for pay-per-view sales, potentially exceeding the previous high of £102 million, and British viewers can watch the event for £19.95 through Sky.

Rory McIlroy, the World No. 1 golfer, participated in a game of Circular Soccer, a new football concept, against Jamie Dornan, a co-star from "Fifty Shades of Grey." McIlroy emerged victorious with a score of 2-1, scoring the winning goal from a long-range shot. The innovative game, designed to enhance skills and ball control, was developed by Dornan's father, medical professor Jim Dornan, and has already been incorporated into FIFA's youth programs and tested by Manchester United's academy.

Colleen Ann Harris, a 73-year-old grandmother, was convicted of first-degree murder for the 2013 shooting death of her third husband, Robert Harris, in Placerville, California, despite claiming traumatic memory loss. This conviction comes decades after she was acquitted of killing her second husband in 1985. The jury reached their verdict in under two hours, with sentencing set for June 5, potentially facing 50 years to life in prison.

Ahead of their highly anticipated boxing match set to be the richest in history, both Floyd Mayweather and Manny Pacquiao spent quality time with their families over the weekend. Manny Pacquiao was accompanied by his wife Jinkee and their five children to Los Angeles, where they recently purchased an £8.4 million mansion in Beverly Hills. Meanwhile, Floyd Mayweather was seen with his partner Doralie Medina, who works for his promotional company and is known online as 'Bad Medina.' Both fighters are now concluding their intense training regimen and will soon enter a lighter phase of preparation, aiming to meet the weight requirement of no more than 154 pounds for their upcoming bout.

Martin Guptill, known for his impressive 237-run innings against the West Indies in the recent World Cup, has been recalled to the New Zealand Test squad for an upcoming two-match series against England. Additionally, young paceman Matt Henry, who has played just 20 first-class matches, has been selected to the Test squad for the first time. Coach Mike Hesson commented that while the squad remains largely stable, there will be opportunities for new talent, highlighting Guptill's potential to perform effectively in longer formats of the game.

During a postgame news conference after Kentucky's loss to Wisconsin in the Final Four, Kentucky guard Andrew Harrison directed an obscenity and racial slur at Wisconsin's Frank Kaminsky, which was broadcast live. Harrison later apologized to Kaminsky, who accepted the apology and stated that the issue was resolved. The incident occurred after a reporter asked about Kaminsky, prompting Harrison to make the offensive remark despite covering his mouth, leading to immediate backlash on social media.

Scottish comedian Limmy shocked his fans by uploading a video showing a large spider emerging from his mouth and crawling across his face, a stunt he humorously dubbed a "bushtucker trial." Despite the revulsion expressed by some viewers, others praised Limmy for creating what they considered the most impressive internet clip ever, while comments questioned how the spider avoided being swallowed. The spider appeared unharmed throughout the seven-second clip as it moved from Limmy's chin up to his cheek.

Andras Janos Vass, a 25-year-old Hungarian, was convicted of human trafficking and racketeering for luring young, gay Eastern Europeans to the U.S. through deceptive online offers of employment, only to force them into sex work. Vass promised high wages and easy jobs to victims he met on dating sites like Planet Romeo, before taking their documents and threatening their families, making them work up to 20 hours daily in captivity across New York City and Miami. Found guilty by a Miami-Dade jury, Vass faces a potential maximum sentence of 155 years in prison, with sentencing set for June 2nd.

Beijing International Airport is expanding with the launch of Terminal 1, the world's largest airport terminal, to accommodate an increasing number of Chinese tourists. Scheduled to open in 2018, the 700,000-square-metre facility, designed by architect Zaha Hadid, will handle up to 45 million passengers annually, emphasizing wide-open spaces and streamlined passenger pathways. This expansion reflects a significant rise in Chinese tourism; in 2014, there were 109 million outbound Chinese tourists spending $164 billion, compared to just 10 million in 2000.

On Thursday, the famous Icebergs Club pool at Bondi was drained for a unique catwalk during Mercedes-Benz Fashion Week Australia, featuring a minimalist, unisex collection from the label Ten Pieces. The show, attended by fashion enthusiasts and beach-goers alike, featured models walking through the empty pool and along the break wall, showcasing ten versatile, monochrome pieces that can be worn in 20 different ways. Hosted by Icebergs restaurateur Maurice Terzini and partner Lucy Hinckfuss, the event took place under clear blue skies and warm temperatures, with guests enjoying snacks and drinks while watching the androgynous designs.

Alain Robert, known as the 'French Spiderman,' successfully climbed Dubai's 1,007-foot Cayan Tower without a safety harness, using only sticky tape and chalk, taking around an hour and a half to reach the top. Despite the risks, the 52-year-old climber completed the daring feat with permission from the Cayan Group, waving a flag in honor of the developers mid-climb. This stunt adds to his list of achievements, including scaling the Burj Khalifa in 2011, although that climb was done legally with a harness.

Stuart Pearce advocates for giving Manchester City manager Manuel Pellegrini additional time to improve the team's performance, which has declined recently, jeopardizing their chances of securing a top-four finish. Pearce, drawing from his experience with the club, attributes the team's poor form partly to the diminished effectiveness of midfielder Yaya Toure, suggesting that significant changes to the squad may be necessary this summer to return to success. Despite the recent challenges, Pearce expresses hope that Pellegrini will remain in charge, emphasizing the need for stability and squad renewal.

On Easter Sunday morning, a tragic incident occurred in Durham, North Carolina, where a married couple, Derek Lowe (38) and Tina Lowe (33), were struck and killed by an Amtrak train on the railway tracks. The train, carrying 166 passengers, was on its route from Charlotte to New York City and was delayed for approximately two and a half hours following the accident, which took place on property owned by Norfolk-Southern. While no passengers were injured, one passenger fell ill during the delay and received medical treatment. Reports suggest the couple may have been living in a homeless camp nearby.

Paul Gregory, 51, from Bedford, was caught in a case of blue badge fraud after Bedford Borough Council discovered a newspaper report detailing his participation in a 191-mile hike. Despite claiming he could only walk 200 meters and needed the blue badge due to his alleged poor health, Gregory was found to be a member of a mountaineering club and had previously participated in challenging hikes. At Luton Magistrates' Court, he pleaded guilty to dishonestly obtaining a blue badge and was fined £795, ordered to perform 150 hours of community service, and subjected to a one-year supervision order and thinking skills sessions.

Manchester United is exploring the possibility of signing Tottenham Hotspur's goalkeeper Hugo Lloris due to concerns about David de Gea's future, but faces a £35 million obstacle as Spurs have no intention of selling their star player, especially to a domestic rival. Known for his tough negotiation tactics, Tottenham chairman Daniel Levy aims to maintain Lloris at the club, having secured him on a five-year contract last year, and is likely to refuse any offer below the hefty valuation placed on the French international.

During the recent tax filing season, the IRS encountered significant issues with its phone system, disconnecting over eight million callers and allowing only 40% of callers to reach an agent, with many waiting over 30 minutes on hold. IRS Commissioner John Koskinen attributed these problems to budget cuts totaling $1.2 billion since 2010, which forced the agency to divert funds from customer services to meet legal requirements of implementing President Obama’s health law and new reporting regulations.

Former Top Gear presenter Jeremy Clarkson, during a charity auction event in Chipping Norton, humorously remarked that being fired from the BBC allowed him to freely use profanity, a privilege he didn't have while working for the network. The event, which raised funds for the town's lido, attracted hundreds of fans who paid £15 per ticket to see Clarkson, who joked about searching for hosting jobs at the job center until he found this opportunity. Additionally, in a light-hearted moment, he mistakenly identified a male bidder as female, attributing the confusion to his time working with James May.

Undercover police in central London have identified Chilean pickpocket gangs as the most efficient thieves operating in the West End, particularly targeting wealthy tourists. These gangs, typically consisting of three to six members, operate intelligently, blending in as families or businessmen to steal cash and valuables worth hundreds of thousands of pounds. While Romanian and Bulgarian pickpockets are more numerous, making up about 70% and 10% of arrests respectively, the Chilean groups are noted for their sophistication and difficulty in being traced, leading to a significant reduction in thefts in areas like Oxford Street due to increased surveillance and monitoring by plain-clothes officers.

In an accidental vehicle fire at a Las Vegas apartment complex, two children died while two others escaped uninjured after playing with fire inside a parked minivan. The incident occurred on Saturday evening at the Arcadia Palms complex when one of the children inadvertently started a fire. Authorities stated that no criminal charges would be filed due to the involvement of minors and confirmed that the fire was unintentional.Witnesses reported hearing a loud explosion followed by a significant amount of smoke, and a woman, possibly a mother or aunt, was taken to the hospital following the tragic event.

Real Madrid received an electrifying reception from their fans as they arrived at the Santiago Bernabeu for their Champions League quarter-final against Atletico Madrid, with supporters mobbing the team coach and lighting flares. Javier Hernandez's crucial goal in the 88th minute secured a 1-0 aggregate victory, thanks to a pass from Cristiano Ronaldo. This win advances Real Madrid into the semi-finals, where they await the results of the draw to determine their next opponent.

On Wednesday, NBC's Today show made an embarrassing error, misidentifying Saturday Night Live creator Lorne Michaels as "Lauren" during a segment about the 100 most influential people in the world, as published by TIME magazine. The interview featured Michaels, who is celebrated for discovering exceptional comedic talent and was listed in TIME's Titans category alongside other notable figures. Corrected versions of the broadcast were aired in subsequent time zones and online, reflecting Michaels' legendary status at NBC and the significance of his contributions to entertainment.

During an Easter Sunday service at the Korean Union United Methodist Church in Rahway, New Jersey, the roof collapsed, leading to the hospitalization of 14 people, including one woman with a serious head injury who was taken to a trauma center. Around 75 people were inside when the ceiling gave way, causing chaos as churchgoers sustained various injuries such as cuts, bruises, and abrasions. No fatalities occurred despite the incident, which is being investigated for its cause, with experts needing to assess the building's stability before allowing anyone to return.

During her annual Easter vacation in the Mediterranean island of Ischia, Germany's Chancellor Angela Merkel faced cooler temperatures around 12 degrees Celsius, in contrast to the warmer UK climate. Staying at the luxurious five-star Miramare Spa Hotel, Merkel, disappointed by the lack of sunshine, engaged in Pilates sessions and enjoyed the hotel's thermal baths while dining on vegetarian meals. Her husband, chemistry professor Joachim Sauer, maintained his low-profile, avoiding media attention as usual.

Radamel Falcao, Manchester United's striker, highlighted Manchester City's Sergio Aguero as his side's biggest threat ahead of the upcoming Manchester derby. Falcao, who participated in a Puma event alongside Aguero, noted that the Argentine has been particularly dangerous against Manchester United, scoring six times since joining City in 2011. Despite playing a limited role under Louis van Gaal, Falcao expressed contentment with his situation at Manchester United, emphasizing his commitment to training and adapting to different playing styles.

Andy Murray recently enjoyed a victorious week both personally and from the sidelines as he attended Barcelona's Champions League match against PSG, where the team secured a 2-0 win thanks to Neymar's goals. This visit marks the second time within less than a week that Murray has been at the Nou Camp, having previously watched Barcelona play Valencia where Lionel Messi scored his 400th career goal. During his stay in Barcelona, Murray is also dedicating time to his clay court tennis training with Jonas Bjorkman.

Tennis legend Martina Navratilova has ended her coaching partnership with former Wimbledon finalist Agnieszka Radwanska after a short collaboration. The duo announced their decision on Friday, following their December announcement of working together. Navratilova's inability to fully commit to the role led to the termination of their agreement, with Radwanska expressing gratitude for the opportunity to learn from such a renowned figure, despite the partnership's limited duration.

On St George's Day, celebrated annually on April 23rd, several prominent current and former English football players used social media to express their pride. Harry Kane shared a nostalgic photo of himself as a youth, while Rio Ferdinand posted an image from his playing days, celebrating a goal against South Africa. Additionally, other footballers such as Gary Cahill, Nathaniel Clyne, Charlie Austin, and Peter Crouch contributed to the celebrations by posting messages and videos reflecting their patriotism and joy on the occasion.

Cristiano Ronaldo received a special commemorative shirt from Real Madrid president Florentino Perez to celebrate reaching 300 goals for the club in just 288 games. The Portuguese forward, who joined Real Madrid in 2009 for a then-world record fee, achieved this milestone by scoring against Rayo Vallecano and now trails only Raul and Di Stefano in the club's all-time scoring list. However, Ronaldo will face Malaga without fellow striker Karim Benzema, who suffered a knee injury during the midweek Champions League match against Atlético Madrid.

Fernando Hierro, Real Madrid's assistant coach and former club legend, asserts that the team can secure back-to-back Champions League titles due to their inherent competitive nature and confidence. Hierro points out that the current squad possesses the strength and determination to achieve success similar to previous eras, especially given their recent victory in last year’s final against Atlético Madrid. He emphasizes that Real Madrid has the potential to break records, driven by a strong belief in their capabilities and leadership under Carlo Ancelotti.

Chelsea are favored to sign Brazilian forward Kenedy, aged 19, as Fluminense has agreed to sell his economic rights to an agency managed by Giuliano Bertolucci, who also represents several Chelsea players. Additionally, former Chelsea player Deco represents Kenedy, providing another advantage for the Blues. Despite interest from other top clubs like Manchester United, Barcelona, and Juventus, Chelsea appear to be leading the race for the young talent, who has impressed while playing for Brazil's youth teams.

Atletico Madrid's striker Mario Mandzukic sustained injuries, including a cut to his nose and an ankle injury, during a tough Champions League quarter-final first leg match against Real Madrid, which ended in a 0-0 draw. Despite these injuries, coach Diego Simeone expects Mandzukic to recover in time for the upcoming second leg match at the Bernabeu. Mandzukic's fitness status is crucial as he competes with Fernando Torres for a starting spot, alongside Antoine Griezmann, who recently demonstrated strong form by scoring both goals in a league victory against Deportivo La Coruna.

Italian authorities initiated a large-scale anti-terrorism operation targeting a cell linked to Al-Qaeda, arresting suspects planning attacks both within Italy and abroad, including in Afghanistan and Pakistan. Wiretaps revealed that some of the suspects had direct contact with Osama bin Laden and discussed potential attacks on the Vatican. The operation, involving raids in seven provinces, led to the arrest of 18 individuals, mostly from Pakistan, and uncovered plans related to a 2009 bombing in Pakistan and human trafficking activities.

Real Madrid are keen on acquiring Manchester United's goalkeeper, David De Gea, and are willing to wait until next summer if negotiations fail this transfer window. This comes as De Gea's performance has been exceptional this season, contrasting with the perceived unreliability of Real Madrid's current keeper, Iker Casillas. Meanwhile, should De Gea remain at Old Trafford, Real Madrid may consider signing Chelsea's Petr Cech, whose contract also expires soon, as a potential replacement.

Ben Flower returned from a six-month suspension due to a punch thrown during the previous Grand Final, receiving a standing ovation despite believing he did not deserve it as he felt he had let his team down. While surprised by the warm welcome from fans, Flower acknowledged their support while expressing remorse over his actions. He aims to return to playing aggressively but emphasized the importance of controlling his emotions to avoid future misconduct.

Wildlife rescue volunteer Sandie Konopelski, aged 58, was fatally struck by a St Louis MetroLink train in southwestern Illinois while responding to a call about an opossum on the tracks. The incident occurred near an Illinois train station around 8 am, leading to her death from blunt-force trauma. A dedicated wildlife rehabilitator licensed by the Illinois Department of Natural Resources, Konopelski was known for her frequent assistance with animal-related emergencies, yet the exact circumstances that led to her not hearing the approaching train remain unclear.

Kevin Rebbie, a 56-year-old from Limerick Township, Pennsylvania, was arrested after a 15-year-old girl discovered a hidden camera in her bathroom, which police traced back to him. The investigation revealed that Rebbie allegedly sexually abused the girl for four years, starting when she was nine, and filmed these acts along with other individuals using the bathroom. He faces multiple charges including aggravated indecent assault, unlawful contact with a minor, and invasion of privacy, after law enforcement found 41 videos captured over several years, many depicting the victim and others while they were undressed or showering.

Aaronessa Keaton, a 24-year-old woman from Phoenix, Arizona, was arrested and charged with manslaughter for a 2014 crash that resulted in the death of a 5-year-old child. At the time of the collision, Keaton, who was eight months pregnant, had marijuana and benzodiazepines in her system. She showed no remorse during her court appearance, responding to news of the child's death with a callous statement. Both the infant born from Keaton's emergency C-section and the deceased child tested positive for drugs, highlighting the severe consequences of her actions.

Football Association chairman Greg Dyke returned a £16,000 Parmigiani watch given to him as a gift during the World Cup in Brazil after initial refusal due to its planned donation to Breast Cancer Care. The return was prompted by FIFA's threats of potential sanctions, leading to the closure of proceedings by FIFA's Ethics Committee. All of the gifted watches will now be auctioned for a Brazilian charity, while Dyke will also make a separate donation to Breast Cancer Care.

Nedum Onuoha, a QPR centre-back with 15 months left on his contract, is being sought after by Hull City, though he also interests Everton and West Ham. Additionally, Hull City is eyeing West Bromwich Albion striker Victor Anichebe, who might be sold this summer. Meanwhile, QPR is considering signing Catania defender Nicolas Spolli, who has been on loan at Roma.

The documentary film "Kid Kulafu" provides an insight into Manny Pacquiao's early life, showcasing his journey from poverty to becoming a renowned boxer. Directed by Paul Soriano, the film, which premiered in Manila, highlights Pacquiao's determination and faith that led him to success despite humble beginnings. With its release coinciding with Pacquiao's upcoming high-stakes fight against Floyd Mayweather Jr., the movie also underscores how Pacquiao's early hardships contributed to his resilience and strength in the ring.

Floyd Mayweather Jnr is diligently preparing for his upcoming bout with Manny Pacquiao by engaging in rigorous training sessions, including swimming and underwater exercises. Meanwhile, Manny Pacquiao has been pushing himself physically by climbing mountains as part of his training regimen. The highly anticipated fight between the two legendary boxers, scheduled for May 2 in Las Vegas, is expected to break records for the highest pay-per-view revenue in boxing history.

At the Laureus World Sports Awards in Shanghai, Benedict Cumberbatch, known for his role as Sherlock, faced off in a table tennis match against Chinese legend Deng Yaping. Despite her kind gesture of playing with a wooden spoon to level the playing field, Cumberbatch, not known for his ping pong skills, lost the match. The humorous skit delighted the audience, showcasing both Cumberbatch's charm and Deng's legendary skill.

Manchester City's players stand to lose £500,000 each in bonuses if they fail to secure a Champions League spot this season due to their incentivized contracts designed to comply with financial fair play regulations. This comes amid their recent losses, including the 1-0 defeat to Manchester United in the derby, which put their European qualification in jeopardy. With only four points now separating them from rivals Liverpool, securing a top-four finish is crucial for the club, as it not only affects their upcoming bonus structure but also their ability to attract high-profile signings like Juventus' Paul Pogba in the next transfer window.

The April 8, 2015 CNN Student News episode covered several topics including California's worsening drought which could have broader national impacts, a flu outbreak affecting dogs in Chicago, and plans for a new tunnel connecting Denmark and Germany through an undersea route. The segment also invited schools to participate in the CNN Student News Roll Call by submitting their details for a chance to be featured in future broadcasts.

In Hong Kong, there's high demand for Jenny's butter cookies, with tourists and locals queuing for hours despite the high price of $9 per tin. This popularity has led to counterfeit versions being sold, prompting the original store to issue warnings about fake products. The craze reflects broader trends of food mania, where items like cronuts and ramen burgers也曾引起人们的狂热追捧，但这种对黄妮蝴蝶酥的狂热是否物有所值还有待商榷。不过需要注意的是，最后那句关于cronuts和ramen burgers是否值得的问题没有直接包含在前文的总结中，因此可以简化为：这种现象反映了世界各地人们对特定食品狂热追捧的趋势。

Kevin Coulton, a 16-year-old student from Manchester, suffered a bruised face after attempting the "Kylie Jenner" challenge, a trend where young people suck their lips into glasses or bottles to mimic Kylie Jenner's fuller lip appearance. Despite the popularity of the challenge among teens, Coulton now advises his peers against participating due to the pain and long-lasting bruising it caused him. The trend has led to numerous instances of young people experiencing unwanted bruising and swelling instead of achieving the desired full-lip effect.

Bristol City is on the verge of securing promotion to the second tier of English football, needing only a victory over Bradford to achieve this feat following a 1-1 draw with Preston North End, despite trailing early due to Jermaine Beckford's goal. Manager Steve Cotterill emphasized the importance of preventing a defeat against Preston, as it would have complicated their chances of promotion if they failed to score points against Bradford. Cotterill's team maintains an eight-point lead over Preston, who remain strong contenders for the second automatic promotion spot, though both managers urge their teams to avoid complacency.

US President Barack Obama stated his firm commitment to ensuring Israel retains a military advantage over Iran, addressing criticisms from Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu regarding the nuclear deal with Iran. Obama explained that the agreement includes measures such as reducing Iran’s centrifuges and limiting uranium enrichment, alongside increased inspections, designed to prevent Iran from obtaining nuclear weapons for an extended period. Despite doubts about Iran's trustworthiness, Obama argued that the deal allows for greater oversight of Iran's nuclear activities, providing assurance that the country cannot develop a nuclear weapon under the terms of the agreement.

During his appearance on "The Tonight Show," Chris Christie confronted Jimmy Fallon about the frequent weight-related jokes, leading Fallon to apologize by offering Christie his favorite treat: a special edition of ice cream named "The Tonight Dough." Despite Fallon's initial suggestion to share, Christie kept the ice cream for himself, indicating he wouldn't alter himself to fit into a presidential role. When asked about his potential presidential run, Christie emphasized he would remain true to himself and mentioned he already had a demanding job.

Manchester City's captain Vincent Kompany, manager Manuel Pellegrini, and head of youth development Patrick Vieira attended the FA Youth Cup final first-leg match where the club's Under 18s suffered a 3-1 defeat against Chelsea. The match, held at the Academy Stadium, highlights Chelsea's strong youth development, with goals scored by Tammy Abraham and Dominic Solanke securing a significant advantage for the London-based team, despite Manchester City's brief equalization. This outcome underscores ongoing debates about the effectiveness of Manchester City's investment strategy compared to Chelsea's focus on developing young talent internally.

In a bizarre drug trafficking operation, a suspect attempted to smuggle $30,000 worth of methamphetamine by stuffing it into an Easter bunny and mailing it to Carolyn Ross in Tahlequah, Oklahoma. The package was intercepted thanks to a police dog's alert in Tulsa County, leading to Ross's arrest when she attempted to claim the package. Ross confessed to authorities that she was aware the package contained drugs and had planned to redistribute them further.

Swansea City have presented a contract offer to Marseille striker Andre Ayew, who is a free agent this summer and has also drawn interest from Newcastle, Everton, Wolfsburg, and Borussia Dortmund. Additionally, Swansea are monitoring Schalke's full-back Christian Fuchs, who is also set to become a free agent and has previously received interest from Sunderland, Hull, and Fulham, alongside his current club's attempts to extend his contract.

Jack Wilshere thanked supporters via social media after captaining Arsenal's U21 team in a recent match against Reading, where they suffered a defeat despite the appearances of players like Serge Gnabry and Abou Diaby. The England midfielder, who has been out since November due to injury, expressed gratitude for the fan support but acknowledged disappointment in the result, aiming to make a return to the first team for the remainder of the season including the FA Cup semi-final.

Vincent Kompany, current captain of Manchester City, shared that his mother prevented him from joining Manchester United when he was 17, insisting he complete his schooling first. Despite Sir Alex Ferguson's interest, Kompany later signed with Manchester City in 2008, expressing pride and passion for the club where he has since won two Premier League titles. Now facing his former potential club in an upcoming derby, Kompany believes it represents an evenly matched contest between two major clubs.

Floyd Mayweather has been spotted weighing in at 150.5 lbs, just 3.5 pounds above the 147 lb welterweight limit, more than three weeks ahead of his high-stakes fight with Manny Pacquiao. Both boxers are required to comply with strict weight guidelines set by the WBC to ensure safety and fair competition. Mayweather's rigorous conditioning and recovery methods, including the use of a Cryotherapy Chamber, are helping him stay on track for the May 2 fight at MGM Grand in Las Vegas, where he aims to maintain his undefeated record.

Veteran handler Billy Collett at Gosford's Australian Reptile Park successfully extracted nearly 500 milligrams of venom from a four-meter-long king cobra named Raja, marking the first time he has milked this species. The massive yield, capable of filling a shot glass and being forty times more potent than that of a brown snake, poses significant danger; it contains enough toxin to potentially kill a full-grown elephant. This venom will be distributed to various research institutions for further study. The procedure also allowed Collett to safely assess the snake's weight and measurements, which had not been done since its last evaluation four years ago.

In the second Test against the West Indies in Grenada, Joe Root scored 182 runs but was denied the opportunity to reach a double century due to a mistake by teammate James Anderson. Root was frustrated as Anderson carelessly failed to run for the second run that Root had called for, leading to Jason Holder catching the ball and removing the bails, causing Root to express his disappointment by throwing down his helmet and gloves. Despite the setback, Root's performance helped England secure a 165-run lead heading into the second innings.

An old hoax video showing a surfer riding waves in front of Sydney's Opera House resurfaced online and went viral for the second time, confusing internet users. Initially posted in 2012 by Darren Johnson after severe storms, the video was shared again this week, coinciding with recent heavy rainstorms in Sydney. Experts confirmed the video's inaccuracies, stating that such waves breaking so close to the Opera House is theoretically impossible due to the geographical layout of Sydney Harbour.

Manny Pacquiao trained outside in Griffith Park, Los Angeles, jogging and shadow boxing alongside fitness enthusiasts as he prepares for his upcoming fight against Floyd Mayweather. The 36-year-old Filipino boxer, accompanied by his dog Pacman, focused on improving his stamina and core strength while showing off his footwork to spectators. Additionally, Pacquiao showcased a vibrant mouthguard that symbolizes his confidence for the highly anticipated $300 million fight scheduled for May 2 in Las Vegas.

In a charming video from Bahama, North Carolina, three dogs—Husky Sky, Springer Spaniels Sadie and Marshall—perform a harmonious howling ensemble for their owner. The performance begins when Sadie starts barking, prompting Sky to respond with a melodious howl. Soon, both Sadie and Marshall join in, creating a joyful cacophony that continues until the owner gently interrupts them, leaving the dogs surprised and seemingly disappointed.

West Ham United is considering signing Javier "Chicharito" Hernandez from Real Madrid, who is currently on loan from Manchester United, due to his recent goal-scoring form that helped Real advance in the Champions League. Hernandez, whose contract with Manchester United expires next year, is expected to cost around £7 million and is surplus to requirements at Old Trafford under Louis van Gaal. Besides Hernandez, West Ham is also looking at younger players including Spanish midfielder Pedro Obiang, Senegalese striker Moussa Konate, and Swiss center-back Fabian Schar, as part of their summer recruitment efforts before moving to their new stadium.

A coach caught fire and erupted into flames on the A2 slip road to the M25 near Dartford, Kent, causing significant disruption. Fire crews responded quickly and worked for about an hour to extinguish the blaze, which was confined to the engine compartment and then spread throughout the vehicle. The incident resulted in the closure of the slip road, and although only the driver was present, no injuries were reported; however, the coach was completely destroyed, and drivers are advised to avoid the area due to ongoing cleanup operations.

Grupo OAS, facing financial difficulties due to a corruption scandal involving Petrobras, is attempting to sell its stake in two World Cup stadiums in Brazil—Natal's Dunas Arena and Salvador's Fonte Nova. These financial troubles stem from an economic downturn, government austerity measures, and currency devaluation, forcing the company to divest from these arenas and refocus on core construction activities. The situation reflects broader issues in Brazilian football post-World Cup, including declining attendance at these venues and some clubs' dissatisfaction with stadium management.

Alison Saunders, the Director of Public Prosecutions, faced scrutiny after it was revealed that her legal adviser, Neil Moore, worked in the same chambers as the son of Lord Janner, who is accused of child abuse. This led to concerns over potential conflicts of interest, especially given that Lord Janner was not prosecuted despite previous allegations, partly due to his advanced Alzheimer's disease. Critics, including MP Simon Danczuk and senior figures like David Davis, questioned the decision and called for a judicial review, casting doubt on Saunders' ability to continue in her position.

Charlie Austin, the prolific QPR striker who has scored 17 goals despite his team's relegation struggles, is under consideration for a potential England call-up by manager Roy Hodgson. This comes as Harry Kane is unavailable due to commitments with the Under-21s, and Theo Walcott's trial did not yield desired results. With Austin impressing throughout the season, Hodgson and his assistant, Ray Lewington, plan to monitor him closely before making decisions for upcoming friendlies and Euro 2016 qualifiers.

The return of "Game of Thrones" has led to a significant increase in online piracy, with over 100,000 daily illegal downloads and more than seven million episodes downloaded worldwide between February and April. Brazil emerged as the top country for illegal downloads, followed by France, the U.S., Canada, and the UK. HBO responded to the piracy issue by announcing that the new season would air simultaneously in 170 countries and launched a new streaming service, HBO Now, aimed at curbing piracy and protecting revenue from such content theft.

During his tenure at Manchester United, former defender Patrice Evra was told by Sir Alex Ferguson that he had the potential to become an excellent coach, alongside teammate Ryan Giggs. Before leaving Manchester United on a free transfer in 2014, Evra pursued some coaching qualifications, aligning with Ferguson’s belief in his managerial capabilities. Currently at Juventus, Evra expresses his intention to obtain his full coaching diploma, continuing along the path laid out by the legendary manager.

The article highlights new research suggesting that genetic factors, specifically a gene called Nox3 found in the inner ear, play a significant role in determining a person's vulnerability to noise-induced hearing loss. This condition is particularly prevalent among military personnel and is one of the most common disabilities among veterans. The study, conducted by researchers at the University of Southern California and published in PLOS Genetics, could allow individuals genetically predisposed to hearing loss to take preventive measures before exposure to dangerous noise levels. Understanding these genetic factors could help in developing strategies to prevent hearing loss rather than just treating it after it occurs.

Susie Clark, an Arkansas resident, discovered a 3.69-carat white diamond at Crater of Diamonds State Park on her last day of a visit with her husband. Clark, inspired by a prayer asking for divine assistance, named the sizable, teardrop-shaped diamond the "Hallelujah Diamond." This discovery marks the largest diamond found in the park since April 16, 2014, and it is one of 122 diamonds uncovered this year within the park's 37.5-acre search area.

A magistrate denied a request by Brett Anthony O'Connor's legal team to prohibit media outlets from identifying him, despite his lawyer arguing it would cause him undue stress and embarrassment. O'Connor, formerly a child safety officer at Education Queensland, faces ten charges including sexual offenses against minors that allegedly occurred over 25 years ago in Sydney. The magistrate agreed to suppress the identities of the victims and witnesses but allowed media to name O'Connor, who was released on bail in March after being charged with assaulting two 12-year-old boys at different Sydney schools.

David Seaman, now 51, recalls his most impressive save as one he made 12 years ago during an FA Cup match against Sheffield United at Old Trafford, where he deflected a header that seemed destined for the net. This save, Seaman notes, surpasses even his penalty-saving feats for England, including those from Euro 96. The former Arsenal goalkeeper also cautions his old team against underestimating Reading ahead of an upcoming FA Cup semi-final, emphasizing the unpredictability and importance of never taking anything for granted in football.

Burger King agreed to cover the expenses and provide gifts for the wedding of Joel Burger and Ashley King, a couple from Illinois who have been called "Burger-King" since childhood. The fast-food chain offered to pay for the wedding and supply personalized gift bags, Mason jars, and Burger King crowns when the couple accepted the proposal during a Skype call. The company's spokesperson expressed joy at honoring the couple's unique connection by organizing a distinctive wedding for them.

Polish police are seeking assistance in identifying a mysterious English-speaking man found in Gniezno, Poland, who claims to have no recollection of his identity or origin. Standing at approximately 6ft3in and around 35 years old, the man was admitted to a psychiatric unit after being found disoriented and wandering into traffic. Despite knowing a few words in Polish and other languages, he communicates primarily in English and remains unaware of how he arrived in Poland or his true nationality. Authorities have released images and details about the man in hopes that someone will recognize him and provide clues to his identity.

Raheem Sterling has acknowledged he is hesitant to sign a new £100,000 weekly contract offered by Liverpool, despite previously expressing readiness to accept a lower salary. The young winger, who has been one of Liverpool's standout players, has underperformed this season, scoring only six goals — one less than Stoke City striker Jon Walters. This decline in form is reflected in a reduced conversion rate and a higher minutes-per-goal ratio compared to his previous performance.

On Wednesday afternoon, a significant fire broke out at a former strip club location, The Body Shop, in Flint, Michigan.
