The blaze, which began around 1pm, caused substantial damage to the building while only affecting nearby power lines. Local resident Terry Rauh mentioned that this was the second time the building had experienced a fire. As of now, an investigation is underway to determine the cause of the incident.

Ashley Young, Manchester United's key player in their 4-1 derby victory over Manchester City, celebrated his team's dominance by mocking their rivals as 'noisy neighbors'. Young, who scored one goal and assisted two others, praised the vocal support from the home fans, noting that the team aimed to silence City's supporters by quickly turning around the match after falling behind early. Following this significant win, Young expressed confidence that United could continue their strong form and secure a successful end to the season.

The article highlights a growing issue of back and neck pain affecting younger individuals, with nearly half of those under 30 reporting persistent discomfort. Experts attribute this trend to sedentary behaviors and excessive use of smartphones, leading to poor posture and increased pressure on spinal discs. They emphasize the need for regular breaks and better posture management to alleviate the tension caused by long hours of sitting, especially while using devices like laptops and mobile phones.

David Villa, despite joining New York City FC after a brief stint at Atletico Madrid, showed no ill feelings towards his former club by cheering them on during their Champions League quarter-final match against Real Madrid. The game ended in a 0-0 draw, with Atletico's goalkeeper Jan Oblak putting in a crucial performance. Villa, now a club captain, celebrated his recent successful match where he scored and assisted a goal, and looks forward to playing alongside fellow star Frank Lampard, who is set to join the team soon.

In a video that has garnered over 100,000 views, Casey Levi attempts to get his son Sam to eat a California roll by offering a $10 prize, but Sam backs down after a minute, expressing disgust and fear of vomiting. In contrast, Sam's younger sister Charlie eagerly accepts the challenge, managing to eat the sushi despite initial difficulty, and triumphantly receives her prize, leading her father to humorously point out Sam's defeat. The video showcases differing reactions to sushi, with many viewers finding the children's responses endearing while some relate to Sam’s aversion to the dish.

CCTV footage revealed a heartbreaking incident where six-year-old Nurhayada Sofia fell to her death after being dragged by an escalator at the Kenanga Wholesale City mall in Pudu, Malaysia. While shopping with her mother and sister, Nurhayada played with the handrail, leading to her being dragged over the side and falling five floors to her death. Her father, who was on the phone with her mother at the time, received the devastating news and an investigation has been initiated by local authorities to examine if the mall adhered to safety regulations.

Fabian Orellana, a forward for Celta Vigo, received a one-match suspension from the Spanish football federation due to his red card incident where he threw grass at Barcelona midfielder Sergio Busquets during a match that ended in a 1-0 victory for Barcelona. The unusual event occurred when Orellaná became frustrated with Busquets' time-wasting and threw a clump of turf at him; although the grass hit Busquets on the neck, not the face, Orellana was still sent off by the referee. As a result, Orellana will miss Celta Vigo's upcoming match against Granada.

In Serie A, Fiorentina secured a 2-0 victory over Sampdoria on a wet pitch, with Alessandro Diamanti and Mohamed Salah scoring the goals, moving Fiorentina to fourth place with 49 points. Additionally, Roma won 1-0 against Napoli thanks to Miralem Pjanic's goal, while Juventus maintained their lead by defeating Empoli 2-0. Other matches saw AC Milan secure their first away win in five months, and Cesena surprisingly earned a point after trailing by three goals in their match against Verona.

Adele Berry, a Vodafone customer, complained to the company after receiving flirtatious texts from a call center employee in South Africa whom she had initially contacted regarding a billing issue. The employee, identified as Miguel, sent numerous unsolicited messages, including one where he called Ms. Berry his "future hubby" and complimented her appearance, despite her lack of any romantic interest during their initial conversation. Vodafone has since suspended the call center worker pending an investigation into the matter.

During the eight-year Afghanistan conflict, the British Army expended 46 million bullets at a cost of £200 million, reflecting the intense combat with the Taliban. This includes 27 million 5.56mm rounds from assault rifles and machine guns, and over 80,000 105mm artillery shells costing £1,250 each. Former soldiers emphasize that the high ammunition usage underscores the daily life-threatening conditions faced by British troops in close combat situations.

Mr. Zhang, a 50-year-old vegetable seller in Dezhou City, China, died after his motorbike was hit by a car at the same intersection where his younger brother had been killed in a similar incident eight months prior. The accident occurred while Mr. Zhang was legally crossing an unauthorized road lacking proper signage, and the driver of the silver car that fled the scene is now under investigation for using a fake license plate. Authorities noted a lack of road safety awareness among the parties involved and urged drivers to exercise caution at junctions.

Firefighter Cody Knecht from St. Tammany Fire District in Louisiana used a duck quacking ringtone to rescue six baby mallards trapped in a storm drain. The video shows him using a pole with his phone attached, emitting a duck call, which lured the ducklings one by one to safety. After a total rescue operation lasting nearly two hours, all six ducklings were eventually reunited with their mother near a canal. This was the second duck-related rescue for the station in just one week.

An 11-year-old boy named Shiva was allegedly beaten to death by his head teacher, Lalit Verma, at Dwarika Prasad School in Railamau village, India, after being caught with another student's pencil and eraser. The attack caused internal bleeding, leading to Shiva's death upon arrival at Fatehpur Hospital. This case highlights a pattern of excessive corporal punishment in some Indian schools, with similar incidents reported elsewhere in the country, including cases where students have died due to severe beatings or abusive treatment by educators.

Charlie Austin shared an amusing anecdote about his initial meeting with teammate Joey Barton at Queens Park Rangers, where Barton initially ignored him before realizing the oversight and subsequently inviting Austin and his fiancée to dinner. Despite this awkward start, Austin and Barton became close friends, with Barton now serving as the club captain. Austin emphasized the importance of securing at least four points in their upcoming matches against West Brom and Aston Villa to avoid relegation, while also expressing amazement at being among the top goal-scorers in the Premier League, alongside stars like Harry Kane and Sergio Aguero.

A nine-year-old boy was severely beaten by his adoptive mother in Nanjing, China, after failing to answer questions about the story of Pinocchio. The abuse included whipping him with a skipping rope and hitting him with metal rods and water pipes, resulting in numerous injuries and scars. After teachers noticed the worsening condition of the boy and his behavioral changes, they alerted authorities, leading to the arrest of the 50-year-old adoptive mother on suspicion of child abuse, while the child is now receiving psychological support and is temporarily with his biological parents.

A secret 1964 police report revealed that an unnamed famous 1960s band frequented a paedophile brothel in Battersea, London, similar to BBC presenter Jimmy Savile who was known to visit the same location regularly in the 1970s. This information emerged during the investigation of Operation Yewtree, led by Detective Superintendent David Grey, after a 2003 report mentioned the brothel's connection to Savile, who was accused of abuse in the 1970s and whose scandal was exposed upon his death in 2011. However, Grey stated that while the visits to the brothel by both the band and Savile suggest a connection, there is insufficient evidence to warrant a deeper investigation without the benefit of

During the annual "Take Our Daughters and Sons to Work Day" at the White House, a young girl named Anya Brodie complimented Michelle Obama on her youthful appearance despite being 51 years old. Impressed and touched by the compliment, Michelle invited Anya on stage to repeat her words and gave her a big hug. The event included children of White House staff, members of the Boys and Girls Club, and D.C. Child and Family Services.

PSV Eindhoven's midfielder Georginio Wijnaldum has hinted at his desire to move to the Premier League, revealing ambitions to play for a top European club. The 24-year-old Dutch international, who has scored 16 goals this season, has expressed interest in playing in stronger leagues like England, citing positive experiences from conversations with teammates currently playing in the UK, including Tim Krul at Newcastle. Wijnaldum acknowledged his happiness at PSV but emphasized his aspirations to compete against higher-caliber players in the Premier League.

The British Army's 77 Brigade, aimed at countering Islamic State's online influence, faces recruitment difficulties due to an inability to attract sufficient numbers of skilled technical experts. Despite initial plans to assemble 2,000 social media and psychological warfare specialists, the brigade is now projected to consist of only 454 regular and reserve troops. Brigadier Alastair Aitken, the brigade commander, acknowledges the need for a more diverse pool of talent with expertise in areas like social media and counter-criminality, indicating potential changes in terms of service to attract such individuals.

A farmer in Anhui Province, China, gained national attention when one of his goats gave birth to a six-legged kid. The unusual "spider goat," which has four forelegs and two hind legs, was born about 20 days ago and is growing well despite initial concerns about its survival. The farmer, Xiao Qibin, noted that the kid is very active and eats with great enthusiasm, although two of its front legs have become shorter and no longer touch the ground.

A garbage truck driver died in Footscray, Melbourne, when his truck rolled forward during early morning bin collection, pinning him against a pole. Additionally, another fatal accident occurred in Launching Place where a car carrying six passengers lost control, rolled over, and crashed into trees, resulting in one death and multiple serious injuries among the occupants, who are all in their 20s.

Scott Stephenson, aged 19, stole an iPhone from a man who was dying of hypothermia after finding him unconscious in the street in South Shields. Instead of seeking help, Stephenson and his friend Dale Walker took the victim's phone and left him there. After pleading guilty to theft, Stephenson failed to attend a previous court hearing, causing distress to the victim's family, and was subsequently arrested. He erupted into a profanity-laden rant outside Newcastle Crown Court upon being re-bailed for sentencing, set for a future date pending a pre-sentence report.

Former football manager Harry Redknapp, who recently left his position at Queens Park Rangers, shared an unpleasant experience where he was verbally abused and hit with coins by some Arsenal fans after a game between Arsenal and Liverpool. Initially welcomed warmly by Arsenal supporters, who greeted him and asked for autographs, Redknapp found himself the target of abuse when traffic delays left him stranded, facing a small but vocal group of detractors among the fan base. Reflecting on the incident, Redknapp emphasized the negative impact such behavior has on the atmosphere at matches, particularly highlighting how a few individuals can spoil the experience for the majority of well-behaved fans.

Per Mertesacker, Arsenal's defender, stated that the team's improved performance this season is due to reintroducing an "arguing culture" after suffering significant defeats last year. This open debate, which includes personal disagreements, helps the team address their shortcomings and find solutions collectively. The shift towards more vocal and constructive confrontation among players has positively influenced Arsenal's mentality and performance, positioning them as strong competitors for the Premier League title.

Miranda Ashley Calvin, a 19-year-old from Gresham, Oregon, was arrested for allegedly hitting 49-year-old Jerrie Ann Hornig with her car and fleeing the scene, resulting in Hornig's death. Calvin was identified and apprehended after a white Mercedes Benz matching the description of the vehicle involved in the hit-and-run was spotted by an individual monitoring police communications. The suspect was subsequently arrested at a nearby hotel, charged with manslaughter, felony hit-and-run, and reckless driving. Hornig's son, Maxwell Horning, expressed his grief on social media, sharing a tribute to his mother whom he described as his "number 1 son."

Slovakia defender Martin Skrtel has dismissed rumors of a potential transfer from Liverpool, stating he intends to stay at the club despite being linked with moves to Wolfsburg and Napoli. With his contract set to expire in 2016, Skrtel's agent confirmed negotiations are ongoing for a contract extension, dismissing claims of any offers from German club Wolfsburg. The Liverpool player, who has made over 200 appearances for the Reds since joining in 2008, remains committed to the club amid a disappointing season that could result in significant squad changes this summer.

In the early hours of Saturday in Orlando, Florida, police intervened to restrain a 20-year-old woman named Britney E. Montenegro who was charged with disorderly conduct after a bar fight spilled onto the streets. Covered in blood that wasn't her own, Montenegro allegedly attempted to attack another person and was subsequently subdued using a restraining technique by law enforcement. Despite her involvement in the brawl, Montenegro emerged with only minor injuries, including a shoulder abrasion whose cause she couldn't explain.

Lucas Moura, Paris Saint-Germain's playmaker, has expressed admiration for Lionel Messi, calling him his idol and praising Messi's playing style as the best in the world. Despite the traditional rivalry between Brazilian and Argentine players, Moura acknowledges the difficulty of facing Messi in PSG's upcoming Champions League match against Barcelona. PSG faces Barcelona first at home and then in the Nou Camp, with Moura emphasizing the need to work hard to limit Messi's impact.

Eligah Christian, 49, led Alaska State Troopers on a high-speed chase after failing to stop when they attempted to pull him over for a $100,000 warrant related to fraud and theft charges. During the chase, Christian drove recklessly and struck several patrol cars before eventually being apprehended at a residence off Pittman Road. He was charged with felony reckless driving and criminal mischief and remains in jail.

At a Sotheby's auction, the iconic 13 glowing blue letters from Yankee Stadium, which were expected to fetch between $300,000 and $600,000, failed to attract any genuine bidders, with only a Sotheby's employee raising a token bid of $260,000. The signage was originally purchased by Hall of Famer Reggie Jackson after the stadium's final game in 2008, with hopes that a new owner would appreciate its historical significance. Meanwhile, a New York Mets bullpen car from the late 1960s unexpectedly sold for $112,500, far exceeding its estimated value of $20,000 to $30,00

Bob Shannon, the former trainer of Ricky Hatton, has been charged with sexually assaulting a minor under the age of 16. Shannon, who assisted Hatton in preparing for his comeback fight in 2012, is known for training Hatton's brother, Matthew, and other boxers who have achieved significant titles. The allegations against Shannon stem from a complaint filed last year and he will face charges in court soon.

Tottenham Hotspur is in negotiations to extend midfielder Nabil Bentaleb's contract, aiming to secure his future due to increasing interest from other European clubs. The Algerian international's performance has attracted attention, prompting Spurs to expedite talks on a new deal that would significantly increase his wages to approximately £35,000 per week. Bentaleb's current contract expires in the summer of 2018, and given his importance to manager Mauricio Pochettino, the club wants to reward him for his recent outstanding performances.

Kenny Dalglish argues that Harry Kane, who recently earned his first senior caps for England, should focus on preparing for Roy Hodgson's full national team rather than participating in the Under-21 European Championship. Dalglish believes Kane's performance against Italy showcased his readiness for the senior squad and contends that playing in the Under-21 tournament would only serve to tire him out before the main European Championships. He emphasizes that the primary purpose of the Under-21 team is to develop players for the senior team, suggesting Kane should be kept fresh for potential inclusion in the Euro 2016 squad.

In a sold-out match at Providence Park, Orlando City secured a crucial victory over the Portland Timbers thanks to goals from former Real Madrid player Kaka and Canadian international Cyle Larin. Larin capitalized on a defensive mistake by Ridgewell, guiding a Kevin Molino cross into the net in the first half, while Kaka added another in the latter stages with a penalty. Despite Darlington Nagbe's efforts to inspire the Timbers, Orlando's defense remained solid, securing their second away win of the season and moving them to third place in the table.

NBC journalist Jeff Rossen captured a unique moment at LaGuardia Airport where an airport worker was spotted doing push-ups on the runway after completing his loading duties. The employee, dressed in his safety gear, performed twelve consecutive push-ups without showing signs of fatigue. Rossen, impressed by the worker’s dedication to fitness, shared a 12-second video on social media, highlighting the individual as "the hardest working man at LaGuardia." The clip quickly gained attention, with many commenting on the necessity of physical strength in handling heavy luggage.

Dirk Kuyt, formerly of Holland, is set to return to Feyenoord for the upcoming season, having signed a one-year contract with the intention of extending his stay. Known for his time at Liverpool and Fenerbahce, Kuyt initially played for Feyenoord from 2003-2006, becoming a fan favorite by scoring the highest in the Eredivisie and earning Dutch footballer of the year in 2006. Feyenoord's director expresses confidence in Kuyt’s ability to continue his career for several more years, comparing him to Manchester United’s Ryan Giggs.

A truck crashed into a car on Interstate 93 in Boston's Dorchester neighborhood around 6am, leading to significant delays for hundreds of morning commuters. Due to safety concerns about the overhanging truck, authorities rerouted subway and rail passengers using shuttle buses, causing long lines and additional travel disruptions. The truck and car drivers suffered non-life-threatening injuries and were hospitalized; the highway experienced massive delays even after the truck was removed by crane at 8:30am.

Liverpool's goalkeeper Simon Mignolet posed for pictures with pilots in an airplane cockpit upon returning to England after his international duty with Belgium in Euro 2016 qualifiers. Despite being part of the national squad, Mignolet did not play due to Thibaut Courtois's preference as the starting goalkeeper. Back in England, Mignolet is set to face Arsenal in a crucial Premier League match, having recently recovered from early-season form issues to become a key player for Liverpool, contributing significantly to the team's improved performance with six consecutive away clean sheets in the league.

The Rangers interim chairman, Paul Murray, and director John Gilligan met with Mike Ashley, owner of both Sports Direct and Newcastle United, in what is seen as a potential step towards resolving the tension between Ashley and the club. This follows Ashley securing an 8.92% stake in Rangers and the ousting of his allies from the Ibrox board. The new board has opted not to draw the remaining £5 million loan from Sports Direct but instead accepted a £1.5 million loan from the Three Bears consortium while planning a long-term funding strategy.

In an exclusive promotional video for BT Sport ahead of Liverpool's FA Cup semi-final against Aston Villa, striker Daniel Sturridge laughed uncontrollably at comedian Darren Farley's spot-on impersonation of former Liverpool star Michael Owen. While Farley's recreation of Owen was impressive, his attempt at Sturridge's celebratory dance fell short. Sturridge's participation in the semi-final is uncertain due to a hip injury, with Aston Villa also facing squad concerns including a potentially unavailable Gabriel Agbonlahor due to a hamstring issue.

A federal appeals court has upheld the conviction and death sentence of Clark Elmore, who was found guilty of raping and killing his 14-year-old stepdaughter in 1995. The 9th US Circuit Court of Appeals confirmed the Washington Supreme Court's decision, rejecting Elmore's claims that his constitutional rights were violated during his trial, including ineffective legal counsel and prejudicial jury perceptions due to his shackling. Although the death penalty remains part of Elmore's sentence, Governor Jay Inslee's moratorium on executions means he is effectively serving a life sentence, with the possibility of further legal appeals to the US Supreme Court.

Swansea City have shown renewed interest in Blackburn Rovers' striker Rudy Gestede, who has scored 20 goals this season, as they aim to strengthen their attack for the upcoming summer transfer window. Additionally, Swansea have also scouted other strikers like Aleksandar Mitrovic from Anderlecht and Obbi Oulare from Club Brugge, while considering an offer for Marseille’s Andre Ayew. Facing Financial Fair Play restrictions, Blackburn must consider selling Gestede to manage their finances, especially since there is also significant interest from larger clubs.

In southern Minnesota, police are investigating the theft of approximately $70,000 worth of bull semen from an unlocked barn on Daniel Weness's farm in LeRoy. The thief stole a canister containing valuable vials of bull semen, each worth between $300 and $1,500, which occurred between April 1st and 7th while the farmer was away on Easter Sunday. Chief Deputy Mark May explained that bull semen is highly valued as it reduces transportation costs for breeding, making it a lucrative target for thieves who understand its market value in improving herd genetics.

Wesley Burton, a beloved 36-year-old father of three and radio host at KPFA in Berkeley, California, died in a hit-and-run accident as he drove home from work early Saturday morning. The incident, which involved a white Dodge Charger crashing into Burton’s silver Mercury, occurred near the Berkeley-Oakland city line, with the suspect fleeing on foot. Burton’s wife Lucrecia, who has pleaded for any witnesses or information about the hit-and-run driver to come forward, stated that he was passionate about being a father and providing stability for his children after growing up without one.

Serge Gnabry, an Arsenal midfielder and Germany Under-21 international, is recovering well from a serious knee injury that sidelined him for over a year. He recently played 90 minutes for Arsenal's Under-21 team against Reading and expressed that he feels "better and better," looking forward to his return to play. Gnabry had previously impressed during the previous season before his injury halted his progress.

Bastian Schweinsteiger, Bayern Munich's holding midfielder, is uncertain to play in their upcoming German Cup quarter-final against Bayer Leverkusen due to an ankle injury sustained during their recent 1-0 win over Borussia Dortmund. Though initially a concern, Bayern has stated that the injury is not severe, yet Schweinsteiger's absence is probable as he did not participate in training on Monday. This adds to Bayern's existing injury woes, which already include Arjen Robben and Franck Ribery among others, and now includes goalkeeper Tom Starke who will be sidelined for four weeks.

Manchester City players, including Vincent Kompany and Lucy Bronze, gathered with over 100 local schoolchildren to unveil a 64-meter mural at the Academy Stadium, celebrating the positive impact of football on global communities. The mural, designed by Manchester artists based on images submitted by City fans worldwide, features scenes of various forms of football play and emphasizes the club's commitment to community engagement through its charitable program, Cityzens Giving. Despite the celebratory event, Manchester City faced a setback on the pitch with a 2-1 loss to Crystal Palace.

Two schoolboys aged around 12 to 13 are being sought by police after they attacked another 12-year-old boy by spraying him with flammable WD40 and threatening to set him on fire in an underpass in Newbury, Berkshire. The victim, who escaped with minor injuries, was approached by the suspects while walking through the subway on Easter Monday afternoon. Thames Valley Police describe the attack as "unprovoked" and are urging witnesses or those with information to come forward to assist in their investigation.

Arsenal is nearing a deal to sign Maxi Romero, a 16-year-old Argentine player compared to Lionel Messi, for around £4.5 million from Velez Sarsfield. Talks between Arsenal and Velez are advanced, with the player expected to continue his development on loan at his current club for at least two more seasons before joining Arsenal. This move aligns with Arsenal's strategy of acquiring promising young talent, as evidenced by their recent signing of defensive midfielder Krystian Bielik.

Dani Alves's agent, Dinorah Santana, announced that Barcelona has presented Alves with a new contract offer as his current deal nears expiration. Several top European clubs, including Manchester United and Liverpool, have shown interest in the Brazilian player, who has been linked with a move away from Barcelona throughout the season. Despite this interest, Santana mentioned ongoing negotiations with the Spanish club, indicating further meetings to discuss the terms of the potential extension.

Steve and Sarah Nick, two school bus drivers from Michigan, allegedly amassed a large weapons cache, including a 50-caliber machine gun and over 17,000 rounds of ammunition, stored in a secret basement bunker. The couple faces 15 felony charges after police raided their home following accusations that they stole more than $50,000 from Sarah's elderly, ill mother to fund the collection. Among the seized items were bulletproof vests, one intended for their young daughter, leading authorities to question the couple's intentions and affiliations with local militias.

During a training session in Russia's Dmitrov district, a hot air balloon crashed into a transmission tower, causing the basket carrying three individuals to plummet to the ground. Despite the terrifying ordeal, the instructor and two passengers were hospitalized with minor injuries and are now in stable condition. A filmmaker captured the entire incident from a car, showing the balloon's collision with the tower, its subsequent disintegration, and the eventual safe rescue of the passengers amidst falling debris.

Conor McGregor, known for his upcoming UFC featherweight championship fight against Jose Aldo, provocatively stated that he could defeat boxing legend Floyd Mayweather within 30 seconds if they faced each other, suggesting that Mayweather's expertise in boxing alone would not be enough to counter McGregor's diverse fighting skills. This claim comes as McGregor prepares for his bout with Aldo, while Mayweather is set to compete in a highly anticipated match against Manny Pacquiao. Previously, another notable boxer, James Toney, met defeat after only three minutes when he challenged Randy Couture in an MMA fight in 2010.

Dr. Alex Zhavoronkov, an anti-ageing expert and director of the Biogerontology Research Foundation, predicts he can live to 150 years due to advancements in medical science and lifestyle knowledge. To achieve this, he adheres to a regimen of regular exercise, drug and supplement use, and frequent health screenings while avoiding marriage, children, and material possessions. Dr. Zhavoronkov is optimistic about upcoming pharmacological solutions that could extend youthfulness and health, enabling him to surpass current life expectancy rates in the UK, which stand at around 79 years for men and 83 years for women.

Manchester City will not allow Valencia to withdraw from their £24 million deal for striker Alvaro Negredo, despite reports suggesting Valencia may want to finance a potential transfer for Radamel Falcao instead. Negredo's failure to celebrate a recent goal has fueled speculation about his future, while Valencia faces financial difficulties affecting its ability to retain key players such as defender Nicolas Otamendi. Additionally, Manchester City had their scouts monitor FC Porto's Brazilian left-back Alex Sandro during a Champions League match against Bayern Munich, indicating interest in the player.

Former British tennis player Greg Rusedski has expressed support for Aljaz Bedene's attempt to overturn an International Tennis Federation (ITF) ruling that currently bars him from representing Great Britain in the Davis Cup. Bedene, ranked 99th in the world, acquired British citizenship recently but previously played for Slovenia in 2010 and 2011, disqualifying him under ITF rules. Rusedski believes Bedene's eligibility could benefit British tennis by motivating other players and improving the team's strength.
